skill.name: error-memory
skill.description: Guides an assistant to record project mistakes in a local error log so repeated failures are easier to avoid.
skill.summary: Record project errors as reusable rules for future assistant work.
skill.author: Aceek

security_audit.summary: The external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance findings are false positives from Markdown content in SKILL.md. The skill does instruct the assistant to append or create a local .claude/errors.md file, so it has low filesystem persistence risk.

low_findings.title: Local Error Log Persistence
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to append to .claude/errors.md and create that file if missing. This is expected behavior, but it can persist project mistakes or user corrections in the workspace.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write behavior is explicitly documented in the process steps. No network transfer, secret harvesting, or hidden execution behavior is present.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported backtick locations are Markdown inline code labels and fenced example content. They do not execute Ruby, shell, or any external command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are plain Markdown in SKILL.md, including type labels and template examples. I found no executable script or command invocation.

low_findings.title: Static Blocker Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography finding at line 3 and reconnaissance finding at line 11 do not match the file content. Line 3 starts the YAML description, and line 11 says to document errors.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain no cryptographic algorithm and no system reconnaissance command. The evidence supports dismissing both static blocker findings.

content.user_title: Record Project Errors for Future Runs
content.value_statement: Repeated assistant mistakes slow down development and reduce trust. This skill turns each mistake into a compact local rule for future work.
actual_capabilities: Classifies mistakes as technical, context, or comprehension errors.
Guides root cause analysis after failures or user corrections.
Defines a compact Markdown table format for reusable error rules.
Instructs assistants to append new entries to .claude/errors.md.
Provides a starter template when the local error log is missing.
limitations: It does not automatically detect errors without assistant judgment.
It writes project notes only when the assistant follows the process.
It does not sync error memory across repositories.
Sensitive project details may be recorded if users do not review entries.

use_cases.title: Prevent Repeated Build Failures
use_cases.description: Capture the cause of a failed build and define a rule before the next change.

use_cases.title: Preserve Project Conventions
use_cases.description: Record corrections about local patterns, file layout, or tooling choices.

use_cases.title: Improve Assistant Handoffs
use_cases.description: Keep a concise mistake log that later Claude, Codex, or Claude Code sessions can inspect.

prompt_templates.title: Record a Simple Mistake
prompt_templates.prompt: I made a mistake in this project. Classify it, find the root cause, and add one concise rule to the error log.

prompt_templates.title: Capture a User Correction
prompt_templates.prompt: The user corrected my approach. Turn the correction into a context rule and append it to .claude/errors.md.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a Failed Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Tests failed after my last change. Identify the root cause, separate symptoms from cause, and record the prevention rule.

prompt_templates.title: Review Before Complex Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Before starting this task, review the existing error log and explain which prior rules affect the implementation plan.

output_examples.input: A test failed because the assistant used the wrong package manager.
output_examples.output: A context error entry noting the wrong tool choice, the missed project convention, and the rule to use the configured package manager.

output_examples.input: The user says the requested behavior was misunderstood.
output_examples.output: A comprehension error entry that states the misunderstood requirement and a rule to restate acceptance criteria before editing.

output_examples.input: A runtime error appears after a syntax change.
output_examples.output: A technical error entry that records the root cause and a future check to run before similar changes.

best_practices: Review each entry before storing sensitive project details.
Keep every rule short, specific, and directly actionable.
Check the error log before making high-risk project changes.
anti_patterns: Do not record vague excuses instead of root causes.
Do not store secrets, credentials, or private user data.
Do not let the error log grow without archiving old entries.

faq.question: Where does this skill store error memory?
faq.answer: It uses a local .claude/errors.md file in the project workspace.

faq.question: Does this skill send data to a server?
faq.answer: No evidence found in SKILL.md of any network request or external service use.

faq.question: Can Codex use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What types of errors does it track?
faq.answer: It tracks technical, context, and comprehension errors.

faq.question: Should users review the saved entries?
faq.answer: Yes. Users should remove sensitive details before keeping entries in the workspace.

faq.question: Does it replace tests or code review?
faq.answer: No. It records lessons learned, but tests and reviews remain necessary.
